mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5pp increase in the unemployment rate over 2 months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in weakening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the consumer price index over 3 months signals an expansionary demand shock that boosts equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansionary demand shock periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the industrial production index over 2 months signals a supply-side expansion that boosts equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in supply-side expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in the federal funds rate and increase in the consumer price index over 3 months signals a monetary policy mistake that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines > 0.5% and CPIAUCSL rises > 2% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in monetary policy mistake periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in the unemployment rate and decline in payroll employment over 2 months signals a labor market shock that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5pp and PAYEMS declines > 1% in 2 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in labor market shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 2% increase in the consumer price index over 3 months signals an expansionary demand shock that boosts equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but only in the regime of monetary easing.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansionary demand shock periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) in the regime of monetary easing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 1% increase in the industrial production index over 2 months signals a supply-side expansion that boosts equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, but only in the regime of early-cycle expansion.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in supply-side expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) in the regime of early-cycle expansion.
